rice.planting: Plant rice in flooded fields. Sow seeds 2-3 cm deep. Space rows 20 cm apart. Best season: June-July (monsoon). Use certified seeds for better yield.
rice.pests: Common pests: Stem borer, leaf folder, brown planthopper. Use neem oil spray — mix 10ml neem oil per liter water. Spray early morning. Remove affected plants immediately.
rice.fertilizer: Apply urea 50kg per acre after 20 days. Use DAP 25kg at planting. Add potash 12kg before flowering. Use organic compost to improve soil health.
rice.harvest: Harvest when 80% grains turn golden yellow. Usually 120-140 days after planting. Cut and dry for 3-4 days. Store in dry place to prevent fungus.
rice.water: Rice needs 5-10 cm standing water during growing. Drain field 2 weeks before harvest. Avoid water stress during flowering stage.

wheat.planting: Sow wheat in November. Seed rate: 40kg per acre. Row spacing: 20cm. Depth: 5cm. Ensure good soil moisture before sowing.
wheat.pests: Watch for aphids, termites, and rust disease. Use neem cake in soil before planting. Spray neem oil if aphids appear. Remove rust-infected leaves.
wheat.fertilizer: Apply urea 50kg, DAP 50kg per acre. Split urea: half at sowing, half after 30 days. Add zinc sulfate 5kg if soil is deficient.
wheat.harvest: Harvest in March-April when grains are hard and golden. Takes 120-150 days from sowing. Thresh immediately after cutting to avoid losses.
wheat.water: Water at crown root initiation (21 days), tillering (45 days), jointing (65 days), and grain filling (90 days) stages.

corn.planting: Plant corn in rows 60cm apart. Seeds 20cm apart in row. Depth: 5cm. Season: February-March or July-August. Use hybrid seeds for better yield.
corn.pests: Common: Fall armyworm, stem borer. Remove affected leaves. Use neem spray weekly. Apply sand in leaf whorl for armyworm control.
corn.fertilizer: Use compost 5 tons per acre. Add urea 60kg in 2 splits: at 20 and 40 days. Apply DAP 50kg at planting.
corn.harvest: Ready in 90-110 days. Harvest when kernels are firm and milky inside. Dry cobs in sun for 5-7 days before storage.
corn.water: Critical water stages: germination, knee-high, tasseling, and grain fill. Avoid waterlogging — corn roots rot easily.

cotton.planting: Sow cotton in May-June after first monsoon rain. Row spacing: 90cm, plant spacing: 60cm. Depth: 3-4cm. Use Bt cotton seeds for pest resistance.
cotton.pests: Major pests: Bollworm, whitefly, aphids. Bt cotton resists bollworm. For whitefly, use yellow sticky traps. Spray neem oil for aphids.
cotton.fertilizer: Apply DAP 50kg, urea 60kg, potash 30kg per acre. Give extra potash during boll formation. Foliar spray of micronutrients helps.
cotton.harvest: Pick cotton when bolls open fully — white fluffy fiber visible. Pick every 15-20 days. Avoid picking wet cotton. Store in dry bags.
cotton.water: Cotton needs moderate water. Critical stages: germination, flowering, boll formation. Avoid waterlogging. Drip irrigation saves 40% water.

sugarcane.planting: Plant sugarcane in February-March or October-November. Use 3-bud setts. Row spacing: 90cm. Depth: 5-8cm. Treat setts with fungicide before planting.
sugarcane.pests: Major pests: Early shoot borer, top borer, pyrilla. Use pheromone traps for borers. Release Trichogramma parasites for biological control.
sugarcane.fertilizer: Apply urea 120kg, DAP 60kg, potash 60kg per acre in splits. Give first dose at planting, second at 60 days, third at 120 days.
sugarcane.harvest: Harvest at 10-12 months. Cane is ready when juice Brix is 18-20%. Cut close to ground. Ratoon crop can be taken for 2-3 years.
sugarcane.water: Sugarcane needs heavy irrigation — 1500-2500mm total. Critical stages: germination, tillering, grand growth. Drip irrigation increases yield by 30%.

tomato.planting: Sow seeds in nursery first. Transplant 25-30 day old seedlings. Row spacing: 60cm, plant spacing: 45cm. Best season: October-November or February-March.
tomato.pests: Common: Fruit borer, leaf curl virus, early blight. Use yellow sticky traps for whitefly. Spray copper fungicide for blight. Remove virus-infected plants.
tomato.fertilizer: Apply compost 10 tons per acre. Add NPK 19:19:19 at transplanting. Give calcium and boron spray during fruiting for quality improvement.
tomato.harvest: Harvest when fruits turn red or pink depending on variety. Pick every 3-4 days. Handle carefully to avoid bruising. Store at room temperature.
tomato.water: Regular watering needed — avoid drought stress. Drip irrigation preferred. Mulching reduces water need by 30% and controls weeds.

potato.planting: Plant potato in October-November in plains, March-April in hills. Use certified seed tubers. Row spacing: 60cm, plant spacing: 20cm. Depth: 5-8cm.
potato.pests: Major: Late blight (most dangerous), aphids, cutworm. Spray Mancozeb fungicide for blight at first sign. Use neem oil for aphids.
potato.fertilizer: Apply urea 60kg, DAP 75kg, potash 75kg per acre. Give half at planting, half at earthing up (30 days). Potash improves tuber quality.
potato.harvest: Ready in 70-90 days. Harvest when leaves turn yellow. Dig carefully to avoid cutting tubers. Cure in shade for 10 days before storage.
potato.water: Potato needs regular moisture. Critical stages: tuber initiation and bulking. Avoid waterlogging — causes rotting. Stop irrigation 2 weeks before harvest.

onion.planting: Sow seeds in nursery. Transplant 6-8 week old seedlings. Row spacing: 15cm, plant spacing: 10cm. Kharif: June-July, Rabi: October-November.
onion.pests: Thrips are major pest — use blue sticky traps. Purple blotch fungal disease — spray Mancozeb. Basal rot — avoid waterlogging.
onion.fertilizer: Apply urea 50kg, DAP 50kg, potash 50kg per acre. Sulfur 20kg improves pungency and shelf life. Avoid excess nitrogen near harvest.
onion.harvest: Harvest when 50% tops fall over. Pull out bulbs and dry in field for 3-4 days. Cure in shade for 2 weeks. Store in well-ventilated place.
onion.water: Regular irrigation needed. Stop watering 2 weeks before harvest for better storage. Drip irrigation reduces disease and saves water.

general.soil_preparation: Plow field 2-3 times. Add organic manure 5-10 tons per acre. Level the field properly. Ensure good drainage. Test soil pH — ideal is 6-7.
general.irrigation: Water early morning or evening. Check soil moisture by hand. If top 5cm is dry, water deeply. Avoid overwatering — causes root rot.
general.organic_pest_control: Neem oil spray: 10ml per liter water. Garlic spray: Crush 100g garlic in 1 liter water, strain and spray. Chili spray: Boil 100g chili in water, cool and spray.
general.composting: Mix kitchen waste, dry leaves, cow dung. Keep moist. Turn every week. Ready in 2-3 months. Use 5kg per plant.
general.soil_testing: Test soil every 3 years. Check pH, nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium. Contact local agriculture office for free testing. Results guide fertilizer use.
general.crop_rotation: Rotate crops every season. After rice, grow wheat or pulses. After cotton, grow wheat. Rotation breaks pest cycles and improves soil health.
general.weather_advisory: Check weather before sowing. Avoid sowing before heavy rain. Harvest before monsoon if possible. Use weather app or call 1551 for Kisan Call Center.

fever.symptoms: Body temperature above 100°F (37.8°C), feeling hot, sweating, weakness, body ache.
fever.home_treatment: Rest completely. Drink plenty of water and fluids. Take paracetamol 500mg every 6 hours. Sponge with lukewarm water. Eat light food.
fever.when_to_see_doctor: If fever lasts more than 3 days, temperature above 103°F, severe headache, difficulty breathing, rash appears, or child under 2 years.
fever.prevention: Drink clean water. Wash hands frequently. Avoid mosquito bites. Get adequate sleep. Eat nutritious food.

cold_cough.symptoms: Runny nose, sneezing, cough, sore throat, mild fever, body ache.
cold_cough.home_treatment: Drink warm water with honey and ginger. Steam inhalation 2-3 times daily. Gargle with salt water. Rest well. Tulsi tea helps.
cold_cough.when_to_see_doctor: If cough lasts more than 2 weeks, blood in cough, high fever, chest pain, or difficulty breathing.
cold_cough.prevention: Avoid cold drinks. Cover mouth when coughing. Wash hands regularly. Avoid crowded places during flu season.

stomach_pain.symptoms: Pain in belly area, cramping, bloating, nausea, loss of appetite.
stomach_pain.home_treatment: Avoid heavy food. Drink warm water. Eat light food like rice, curd, banana. Rest. Take antacid if acidity. ORS for diarrhea.
stomach_pain.when_to_see_doctor: If pain is severe, lasts more than 6 hours, vomiting blood, black stool, or fever with pain.
stomach_pain.prevention: Eat fresh food. Drink clean water. Wash hands before eating. Avoid overeating. Eat at regular times.

headache.symptoms: Pain in head, pressure feeling, sensitivity to light or sound, nausea.
headache.home_treatment: Rest in quiet dark room. Apply cold compress on forehead. Drink water. Take paracetamol if needed. Massage temples gently. Avoid screen time.
headache.when_to_see_doctor: If headache is sudden and severe, with fever and stiff neck, vision changes, after head injury, or recurring daily.
headache.prevention: Get adequate sleep. Drink enough water. Avoid stress. Eat regular meals. Limit screen time.

diabetes.symptoms: Frequent urination, excessive thirst, unexplained weight loss, blurred vision, slow healing wounds, fatigue.
diabetes.home_treatment: Follow prescribed medicines strictly. Eat low sugar, low carb diet. Exercise 30 minutes daily. Monitor blood sugar regularly. Avoid sweets and white rice.
diabetes.when_to_see_doctor: Immediately if blood sugar above 300 or below 70. Regular check every 3 months. Annual eye and kidney check.
diabetes.prevention: Maintain healthy weight. Exercise regularly. Eat balanced diet. Avoid sugary drinks. Get blood sugar tested yearly after age 40.

blood_pressure.symptoms: High BP: headache, dizziness, blurred vision, chest pain. Low BP: dizziness, fainting, weakness, cold skin.
blood_pressure.home_treatment: High BP: Take medicines regularly, reduce salt, exercise, avoid stress. Low BP: Drink more water, eat small frequent meals, avoid standing suddenly.
blood_pressure.when_to_see_doctor: If BP above 180/120 or below 90/60. Chest pain, severe headache, or vision changes — go to emergency immediately.
blood_pressure.prevention: Reduce salt intake. Exercise regularly. Maintain healthy weight. Avoid smoking and alcohol. Manage stress. Check BP monthly.

diarrhea.symptoms: Loose watery stools 3 or more times a day, stomach cramps, nausea, dehydration signs: dry mouth, less urine.
diarrhea.home_treatment: Drink ORS (Oral Rehydration Solution) — mix 1 liter clean water, 6 teaspoons sugar, half teaspoon salt. Eat rice, banana, curd. Avoid spicy food.
diarrhea.when_to_see_doctor: If diarrhea lasts more than 2 days, blood in stool, high fever, signs of severe dehydration, or child under 5 years.
diarrhea.prevention: Drink boiled or filtered water. Wash hands before eating. Eat freshly cooked food. Cover food to prevent flies.

malaria.symptoms: Cyclical fever with chills and sweating every 48-72 hours, headache, body ache, nausea, vomiting.
malaria.home_treatment: Take prescribed antimalarial medicines completely. Rest. Drink plenty of fluids. Take paracetamol for fever. Do not stop medicines early.
malaria.when_to_see_doctor: Immediately if suspected malaria. Get blood test done. Severe malaria with confusion or difficulty breathing needs emergency care.
malaria.prevention: Sleep under mosquito net. Use mosquito repellent. Remove stagnant water near home. Wear full sleeve clothes in evening.

eye_problems.symptoms: Redness, itching, discharge, blurred vision, pain in eye, sensitivity to light.
eye_problems.home_treatment: Wash eyes with clean water. Do not rub eyes. Use clean cloth to wipe. For conjunctivitis, use antibiotic eye drops as prescribed.
eye_problems.when_to_see_doctor: If vision is blurred, severe pain, injury to eye, or symptoms do not improve in 2 days.
eye_problems.prevention: Wash hands before touching eyes. Wear sunglasses in bright light. Eat vitamin A rich foods — carrots, green leafy vegetables.

skin_problems.symptoms: Rash, itching, redness, blisters, dry or scaly skin, swelling.
skin_problems.home_treatment: Keep skin clean and dry. Apply coconut oil for dry skin. For fungal infection, use antifungal cream. Avoid scratching.
skin_problems.when_to_see_doctor: If rash spreads rapidly, fever with rash, pus formation, or does not improve in a week.
skin_problems.prevention: Bathe daily. Wear clean clothes. Avoid sharing towels. Keep skin moisturized. Wear cotton clothes in hot weather.

paracetamol.dosage: Adults: 500-1000mg every 6-8 hours. Maximum 4000mg per day. Children: Consult doctor for weight-based dosing.
paracetamol.precautions: Do not exceed maximum dose. Avoid alcohol. Take with food if stomach upset. Do not use with other paracetamol-containing medicines.

antacid.use: For acidity, heartburn, and stomach upset
antacid.dosage: 1-2 tablets after meals and at bedtime. Or as directed by doctor.
antacid.precautions: Do not take with other medicines — wait 2 hours. Drink plenty of water. Do not use for more than 2 weeks without doctor advice.

ors.use: For dehydration from diarrhea, vomiting, or fever
ors.dosage: Dissolve 1 ORS packet in 1 liter clean water. Drink small sips frequently. Adults: 200-400ml after each loose stool.
ors.precautions: Use clean water only. Prepare fresh every 24 hours. Do not add extra sugar or salt. Continue breastfeeding for infants.

iron_folic_acid.use: For anemia, especially in pregnant women and children
iron_folic_acid.dosage: 1 tablet daily after meals. Pregnant women: as prescribed by doctor.
iron_folic_acid.precautions: Take with vitamin C (lemon juice) for better absorption. May cause dark stools — this is normal. Avoid tea/coffee within 1 hour.

first_aid.cuts_wounds: Wash with clean water for 5 minutes. Apply pressure with clean cloth to stop bleeding. Clean with antiseptic. Cover with clean bandage. Change daily. See doctor if deep or dirty wound.
first_aid.burns: Cool with running water for 10-20 minutes. Do not apply ice, oil, toothpaste, or butter. Cover with clean cloth. Take paracetamol for pain. See doctor if burn is large or on face/hands.
first_aid.snake_bite: Keep calm — panic increases venom spread. Immobilize affected limb. Remove jewelry and tight clothing. Do not cut, suck, or apply tourniquet. Rush to hospital immediately. Note snake appearance if safe.
first_aid.insect_bite: Wash area with soap and water. Apply ice pack for 10 minutes. Take antihistamine tablet for itching. See doctor if swelling increases, difficulty breathing, or allergic reaction.
first_aid.choking: For adult: Give 5 back blows between shoulder blades. Then 5 abdominal thrusts (Heimlich maneuver). Repeat until object comes out. Call 108 if unconscious.
first_aid.fracture: Do not move injured person unnecessarily. Immobilize the injured part with splint or rolled newspaper. Apply ice pack. Call 108 for emergency. Do not try to straighten broken bone.

maternal_health.pregnancy_care: Attend all antenatal checkups — minimum 4 visits. Take iron-folic acid tablets daily. Eat nutritious food. Avoid heavy work. Sleep 8 hours. Deliver in hospital.
maternal_health.danger_signs: Go to hospital immediately if: heavy bleeding, severe headache, blurred vision, fits, baby not moving, water breaks before 37 weeks, or high fever.
maternal_health.newborn_care: Breastfeed within 1 hour of birth. Breastfeed exclusively for 6 months. Keep baby warm. Immunize as per schedule. Weigh baby monthly.

mental_health.stress: Talk to trusted family member or friend. Take breaks from work. Exercise daily. Practice deep breathing. Avoid alcohol. Sleep well. Seek help if feeling hopeless.
mental_health.depression: Feeling sad for more than 2 weeks, loss of interest, sleep problems, fatigue — these may be depression. Talk to doctor. It is treatable. You are not alone.
mental_health.anxiety: Worry, restlessness, fast heartbeat, difficulty sleeping — may be anxiety. Deep breathing helps. Avoid caffeine. Exercise. Talk to doctor if severe.

fraud_awareness.otp_scam: DANGER: Never share OTP with anyone on phone or message. Banks, government, and police NEVER ask for OTP. If someone asks for OTP — it is 100% a scam. Hang up immediately and call your bank.
fraud_awareness.lottery_scam: You cannot win a lottery you did not enter. Do not send money to claim prize. Do not share bank details. This is always a scam. Block the number.
fraud_awareness.fake_call_scam: Banks never call asking for full card number, CVV, or PIN. Do not share these details on phone. Hang up and call your bank directly on the number on your card.
fraud_awareness.link_scam: Do not click links in unknown messages or WhatsApp. They can steal your information and money. Delete suspicious messages. Never enter bank details on unknown websites.
fraud_awareness.impersonation: Scammers pretend to be police, bank officers, or government officials. Verify caller identity before sharing information. Call back on official number. Real officials never demand money on phone.
fraud_awareness.upi_scam: Never scan QR code to RECEIVE money — scanning sends money, not receives. Do not enter UPI PIN to receive payment. If someone says scan to get money, it is a scam.
fraud_awareness.job_scam: Fake job offers ask for registration fee or security deposit. Legitimate companies never ask for money to give job. Verify company on official website before paying anything.
fraud_awareness.investment_scam: Promises of very high returns (50-100%) are always scams. Do not invest in unknown apps or schemes. Verify with SEBI registered advisors only.

emergency_numbers.cyber_crime: 1930 — For online fraud and cyber crime

home_safety.fire_prevention: Do not leave cooking unattended. Keep matchboxes away from children. Check gas connections regularly. Have fire extinguisher. Know escape route from home.
home_safety.electrical_safety: Do not touch switches with wet hands. Do not overload sockets. Check wiring regularly. Turn off appliances when not in use. Use ISI marked products only.
home_safety.water_safety: Boil drinking water or use filter. Store water in clean covered containers. Clean water tanks every 6 months. Do not let water stagnate — breeds mosquitoes.
home_safety.lpg_safety: Check gas pipe connections regularly. Do not use gas if you smell leakage — open windows, do not switch on lights, call gas agency. Keep cylinder upright. Do not store near heat.

road_safety.general: Wear helmet on two-wheeler — it saves life. Wear seatbelt in car. Do not use phone while driving. Follow traffic signals. Do not drink and drive.
road_safety.accident: Call 108 for ambulance. Do not move injured person unless in danger. Keep them warm. Stop bleeding with pressure. Stay with them until help arrives.

time_management.morning_routine: Wake up early. Drink water. Exercise or walk 30 minutes. Eat healthy breakfast. Plan your day.
time_management.medication_schedule: Take medicines at same time daily. Keep medicine box organized. Set reminders. Do not skip doses. Keep record of medicines.
time_management.meal_times: Breakfast: 7-9 AM. Lunch: 12-2 PM. Dinner: 7-9 PM. Eat at regular times. Do not skip meals. Eat slowly and chew well.

communication.making_calls: Speak clearly and slowly. State your name first. Explain purpose of call. Listen carefully to response. Write down important information.
communication.receiving_calls: Answer politely. Verify caller identity. Do not share personal information to unknown callers. Ask to call back if unsure. It is okay to say no.
communication.messages: Keep messages short and clear. Read carefully before sending. Do not share sensitive information via message. Beware of forwarded messages — verify before sharing.

government_schemes.pm_kisan: PM-KISAN gives Rs 6000 per year to farmers in 3 installments. Register at pmkisan.gov.in or nearest CSC center. Need Aadhaar, bank account, land records.
government_schemes.ayushman_bharat: Ayushman Bharat gives free health insurance up to Rs 5 lakh per year. Check eligibility at pmjay.gov.in. Show card at empanelled hospitals for free treatment.
government_schemes.ration_card: Ration card gives subsidized food grains. Apply at nearest ration office. Need Aadhaar, address proof, income certificate. Check status at your state food department website.
